content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `bdf60d60..c6cff9fb`, read the modified diagnostics/resolver code plus related policy/config definitions, and ran `cargo test` and `cargo fmt --check` successfully. I found two remaining correctness issues in the restored `final_reviewer` compatibility path. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing unrelated warnings outside this diff.

## Amendments

1. In `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:1013-1033` and `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:1802-1810`, the restored `final_reviewer` compatibility alias always uses the first configured `final_review.backends` slot (`configured_specs.first()` / `member_views.first()`) instead of the first reviewer that actually survives panel resolution. That breaks valid optional-first configurations. Repro: with `final_review.backends = ["?openrouter", "claude"]` and `min_reviewers = 1`, `backend probe --role final_review_panel --flow standard --json` succeeds by omitting the disabled OpenRouter member, but `backend probe --role final_reviewer` fails and `backend show-effective --json` reports `final_reviewer` as unresolved. The compatibility alias should resolve to the first effective reviewer after optional-member omission, not the raw first config entry.
   Rationale: This patch explicitly restores `final_reviewer` for backward compatibility. Returning an omitted/disabled reviewer still breaks that contract for supported panel configurations and will mislead scripts and operators.
2. In `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:1136-1151` and `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:1337-1361`, availability failures for the restored `final_reviewer` probe still use the generic role metadata path, so the error is reported as `required target 'final_reviewer' (final_reviewer) ... [source: default_backend]` even though `probe_final_reviewer()` resolves reviewer slot 0 from `final_review.backends` in `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:1012-1033`. Repro: make reviewer[0] an enabled OpenRouter slot and unset `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`; the probe fails with the wrong target label and wrong config source. Update `probe_primary_target_label` and `probe_primary_config_source` for `final_reviewer` so availability errors still point to `reviewer[0]` and `final_review.backends`.
   Rationale: When the compatibility probe fails at availability time, it currently sends operators to the wrong config knob and hides which panel member actually failed. That is a real diagnostics correctness bug.
